node.statement: (Structural consequence for extremal NPA solutions). By the main theorem, any extreme point φ of the level-k feasible set satisfies Σ n_j² ≤ m_k + 1, where π_φ ≅ ⊕ σ_j^{⊕n_j} is the GNS decomposition. This provides structural information about optimal quantum strategies: (i) The GNS Hilbert space decomposes as ⊕_j H_j ⊗ C^{n_j} with Σ n_j² bounded. (ii) The total dimension of the representation is bounded by Σ n_j · dim(σ_j), though the dimensions dim(σ_j) of the irreducible representations are not directly bounded. (iii) SDP solvers generically find extreme points of the feasible set, so the SDP solution automatically has this bounded-complexity structure.
